testFiles.description: Tests for the WaveField geometry system

tests.name: init creates waves based on density and maxWaves config
tests.description: WaveField with low density should produce fewer waves than high density, and both should be capped by maxWaves config.
assertions: High density produces more waves than low density
Wave count never exceeds maxWaves config value
tests.approach: Create two WaveField instances with different density values (0.3 vs 1.0) using the same seed. Use a getWaveCount() test helper (analogous to getParticleCount). Verify high density count > low density count. Create a third with maxWaves=8 and density=1.0; verify count <= 8.

tests.name: same seed produces same initial wave configuration
tests.description: Two WaveField instances initialized with the same seed and params should produce identical wave structures.
assertions: Wave positions from same seed are deeply equal
tests.approach: Create two WaveField instances with seed 'deterministic-seed' and defaultParams. Use getWavePositions() helper. Expect deep equality.

tests.name: different seeds produce different wave configurations
tests.description: Two WaveField instances initialized with different seeds should produce different wave structures.
assertions: Wave positions from different seeds are not equal
tests.approach: Create two WaveField instances with seeds 'seed-one' and 'seed-two'. Expect getWavePositions() to not be deeply equal.

tests.name: draw does not throw with valid FrameState
tests.description: WaveField.draw should execute without errors given standard FrameState values.
assertions: draw() does not throw
tests.approach: Init a WaveField, call draw with a standard frame (time: 1000, delta: 16, elapsed: 1000, width: 800, height: 600, params: defaultParams). Wrap in expect().not.toThrow().

tests.name: draw does not throw with edge-case params (zero bass, zero treble, no pointer)
tests.description: WaveField should handle edge cases gracefully: all-zero audio energy, no pointer coordinates.
assertions: No error with bassEnergy=0, trebleEnergy=0, pointerDisturbance=0, no pointerX/Y
tests.approach: Init WaveField with params where bassEnergy=0, trebleEnergy=0, pointerDisturbance=0. Call draw with FrameState lacking pointerX/pointerY. Expect no throw.

tests.name: draw does not throw at boundary parameter values
tests.description: WaveField should render without errors at extreme boundary values for all VisualParams.
assertions: No throw for multiple boundary value combinations (all 0s, all 1s, mixed)
tests.approach: Test boundary combinations: {bassEnergy:0, trebleEnergy:0, density:0, motionAmplitude:0.2}, {bassEnergy:1, trebleEnergy:1, density:1, motionAmplitude:1}, and {curveSoftness:0, structureComplexity:0} vs {curveSoftness:1, structureComplexity:1}. Init and draw each. Expect no throw.

tests.name: bass energy influences wave amplitude
tests.description: Higher bass energy should produce visually larger wave strokes (canvas stroke calls at different positions).
assertions: Canvas draw calls differ between bassEnergy=0 and bassEnergy=1
tests.approach: Spy on ctx.lineTo. Run draw with bassEnergy=0 and collect lineTo y-coordinates. Run again with bassEnergy=1. Compare the y-coordinate ranges (max-min). High bass should produce a larger y-spread across lineTo calls, indicating larger wave amplitude.

tests.name: treble energy influences visual properties (stroke style or line width)
tests.description: Higher treble energy should affect rendering properties like strokeStyle alpha or lineWidth.
assertions: strokeStyle or lineWidth values differ between trebleEnergy=0 and trebleEnergy=1
tests.approach: Spy on ctx strokeStyle setter and lineWidth setter. Draw with trebleEnergy=0, capture values. Draw with trebleEnergy=1, capture values. At least one set of captured values should differ.

tests.name: pointer disturbance warps wave output
tests.description: With non-zero pointerDisturbance and a pointer position, wave draw calls should differ from the zero-disturbance case.
assertions: lineTo calls differ between pointerDisturbance=0 and pointerDisturbance=1
tests.approach: Spy on ctx.lineTo. Draw once with pointerDisturbance=0, pointerX=0.5, pointerY=0.5. Draw again with pointerDisturbance=1, same pointer. Compare captured lineTo coordinates. They should differ.

tests.name: reduced motion (low motionAmplitude) is respected
tests.description: WaveField with motionAmplitude=0.2 should produce less movement over multiple frames than motionAmplitude=1.0.
assertions: Low motionAmplitude produces less visual change across frames than high motionAmplitude
tests.approach: Spy on ctx.lineTo. Draw two frames (time=0 and time=1000) with motionAmplitude=0.2, record lineTo coords diff. Repeat with motionAmplitude=1.0. The total displacement of lineTo y-values between frames should be less for low amplitude.

tests.name: waves use canvas stroke API (beginPath, lineTo, stroke)
tests.description: WaveField should render using stroke-based canvas operations, distinguishing it from particle fill-based rendering.
assertions: ctx.beginPath is called
ctx.lineTo is called multiple times
ctx.stroke is called
tests.approach: Spy on ctx.beginPath, ctx.lineTo, ctx.stroke. Init and draw WaveField. Verify all three were called, and lineTo was called many times (at least wave count * some segment count).

tests.name: wave colors derive from paletteHue and paletteSaturation
tests.description: WaveField should use HSL colors based on the paletteHue and paletteSaturation params.
assertions: strokeStyle values contain HSL color strings
Hue values are in the neighborhood of paletteHue
tests.approach: Define ctx.strokeStyle as a spied property setter. Draw with paletteHue=200. Capture all strokeStyle values. Filter for HSL strings. Verify at least one exists and that parsed hue values are within a reasonable range of 200 (accounting for per-wave hueOffset).

tests.name: no localStorage or cookie access during wave operations
tests.description: Privacy: WaveField must not access persistent storage.
assertions: Storage.prototype.getItem not called
document.cookie getter not called
tests.approach: Spy on Storage.prototype.getItem and document.cookie getter. Init and draw WaveField. Verify neither spy was called. Same pattern as T-009-17.

testFiles.description: Tests for the ModeManager composite geometry system

tests.name: mode manager implements GeometrySystem interface (init + draw)
tests.description: The ModeManager returned by createModeManager should have init() and draw() methods matching the GeometrySystem interface.
assertions: Returned object has init method
Returned object has draw method
Both are functions
tests.approach: Call createModeManager with a registry of two mock modes. Verify the result has init and draw as functions. Call init and draw; verify no throw.

tests.name: initial mode is deterministic from seed
tests.description: Given the same seed, the mode manager should always select the same initial mode.
assertions: Same seed always selects same initial mode
Verified by checking which mock system's init is called
tests.approach: Create two mock GeometrySystem factories. Create two ModeManager instances with same seed. Init both. Track which mock's init was called first (via vi.fn). Both should call the same mock's init.

tests.name: different seeds can produce different initial modes
tests.description: With enough different seeds, the mode manager should eventually select different initial modes, proving seed-based selection.
assertions: At least two different initial modes are observed across multiple seeds
tests.approach: Create two mock mode factories. Try 20 different seeds, track which mode is selected initially. Verify that not all seeds select the same mode (at least 2 different choices observed).

tests.name: mode switches at elapsed time boundary
tests.description: After enough elapsed time passes (seeded interval between 90-180s), the mode manager should switch to a different mode.
assertions: Active draw target changes after elapsed exceeds switch interval
Both mock systems' draw methods are called across the session
tests.approach: Create two mock GeometrySystem objects with spied draw methods. Init mode manager. Draw frames at elapsed=0 (should call mode A draw). Then draw at elapsed=200000 (well past any 90-180s interval). Verify that the second mode's draw was called.

tests.name: both modes receive the same seed on init
tests.description: When the mode manager initializes each mode (at startup or on switch), it should pass the same session seed.
assertions: Both mock systems receive the same seed string in their init calls
tests.approach: Create two mock GeometrySystem factories with spied init methods. Init mode manager with seed 'shared-seed'. Trigger a mode switch by advancing elapsed time. Check that both mock inits received 'shared-seed' as the seed argument.

tests.name: both modes receive same VisualParams
tests.description: The active mode's draw should receive the same FrameState (and thus VisualParams) that was passed to the mode manager's draw.
assertions: FrameState passed to active mode's draw is identical to what was given to manager
tests.approach: Create mock mode with spied draw. Init manager. Call manager.draw with a specific FrameState. Verify the mock's draw was called with the exact same FrameState object.

tests.name: mode switch does not throw
tests.description: The transition from one mode to another should happen without errors.
assertions: No error thrown when draw crosses a switch boundary
tests.approach: Init mode manager. Draw at elapsed=0. Then draw at elapsed=200000 (past switch boundary). Wrap second draw in expect().not.toThrow().

tests.name: mode switch does not break playback — draw continues producing output
tests.description: After a mode switch, the manager should still call the new active system's draw on every frame.
assertions: New mode's draw is called on frames after the switch
No frames are dropped or skipped
tests.approach: Create two mock modes with draw spies. Init manager. Draw multiple frames past the switch point. Verify that after the switch, every subsequent draw call goes to the second mode. Ensure total draw calls on mocks matches total frames drawn on manager.

tests.name: registry accepts multiple modes and cycles through them
tests.description: If more than two modes are registered, the manager should cycle through them in sequence.
assertions: With 3 registered modes and enough elapsed time, all 3 modes are activated
tests.approach: Register 3 mock modes. Set elapsed times to cross two switch boundaries (e.g., 0, 200000, 400000). Verify all 3 mock draw methods are called at least once.

tests.name: switch interval is seeded and varies by seed
tests.description: Different seeds should produce different switch intervals (within the 90-180s range).
assertions: At least two different switch intervals observed across multiple seeds
tests.approach: For multiple seeds, create a mode manager, init it, and draw frames at 1-second intervals. Record the elapsed time at which the active mode changes. Verify that not all seeds produce the same switch time.

tests.name: mode manager works with real ParticleField and WaveField
tests.description: Integration test: mode manager initialized with actual ParticleField and WaveField factories should init and draw without errors.
assertions: init does not throw
draw does not throw before switch
draw does not throw after switch
tests.approach: Import createParticleField and createWaveField. Register both in createModeManager. Create a canvas, init the manager, and draw frames at elapsed=0, elapsed=100000, and elapsed=200000. Expect no throws across all frames.

tests.name: no localStorage or cookie access during mode management
tests.description: Privacy: ModeManager must not access persistent storage.
assertions: Storage.prototype.getItem not called
document.cookie getter not called
tests.approach: Spy on Storage.prototype.getItem and document.cookie getter. Init mode manager with mock modes, draw several frames including a switch. Verify neither spy was called.

helpers: createTestCanvas(): { canvas: HTMLCanvasElement, ctx: CanvasRenderingContext2D } — creates 800x600 canvas via document.createElement
getWaveCount(system): number — analogous to getParticleCount, returns internal wave array length
getWavePositions(system): Array<{y, amplitude, frequency, phase}> — analogous to getParticlePositions
createMockGeometrySystem(): GeometrySystem — returns { init: vi.fn(), draw: vi.fn() } for mode manager tests
imports: import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest'
